DDR5-6400.reason: Spot 7th consecutive ATH $42.833. $43 only $0.167 away (~1 day). +4.22% since 5/20. DDR4 alternative path closing (3-day +2.5%/+3.1%). SK Hynix wage negotiations June formal start adds supply risk.

DDR5-5600.reason: Spot 7th consecutive ATH. DDR4 3-day acceleration exceeding DDR5 confirms full DRAM bull market. DDR4 substitution path closing fastest in 4 weeks. SK Hynix wage negotiations + SSD all-category strain.

PCIe4 TLC.reason: MLC NAND ending THIS MONTH (-41.7% global capacity). PM1733 discontinued + NAND Q1 $46B record (+3.5x) + cumulative contract 4.2-4.5x multiplier + enterprise SSD +48-53%. Five-layer supply contraction. 30TB TLC +472% quantifies market squeeze. DiscTech Day 38 offline.

PCIe5 TLC.reason: 30.72TB CDW dual-channel In Stock Day 3 + CDW-G Day 10 confirms availability improvement. 8-ref 5+ source most reliable column. SS $17,700 Day 28 (6-week+). SHI $21,529 Day 6 upper band. Nvidia Vera Rubin production + COMPUTEX Gen6 announcements strengthen PCIe5 SSD demand trajectory.

PCIe4 QLC.reason: 61.44TB alternative channel network Day 10 double-digit milestone (4 channels, ~34% premium). GovConnection -2.0% first price decrease (positive but minor). Mouser Day 17 irreversible depletion. MLC NAND exit this month creates peak QLC demand pressure.

newEscalatedRisks.risk: 🆕🆕 NAND Q1 2026 revenue $46B (+3.5x YoY) — single quarter exceeds entire 2023
newEscalatedRisks.status: NEW — Counterpoint Research June 3-4, 2026. Historical NAND revenue record.
newEscalatedRisks.detail: Global NAND flash revenue hit $46B in Q1 2026 — 3.5x YoY increase, exceeding entire 2023 full-year revenue. Enterprise SSD = 43% of NAND market, projected >60% by year-end. Samsung 29% share, SK Hynix 18%, YMTC 13% (+5pp YoY). Combined with Counterpoint + TrendForce + Goldman Sachs data, NAND shortage is now quantified at unprecedented scale.

newEscalatedRisks.risk: 🆕🆕 NAND cumulative contract multiplier 4.2-4.5x — enterprise SSD 30TB +472%
newEscalatedRisks.status: NEW — Quantitative milestone. 30TB TLC enterprise SSD tracked $3,062 (Q2 2025)→$17,500 (Q1 2026) = +472%.
newEscalatedRisks.detail: NAND contract prices multiplied 4.2-4.5x from Q4 2025 through Q2 2026 (5 quarters cumulative). Enterprise SSD prices +60% in 2026 alone. A single 30TB TLC enterprise SSD that cost $3,062 in Q2 2025 now costs $17,500 in Q1 2026 (+472%). This is the first quantification of the full supply squeeze magnitude for enterprise SSDs.

newEscalatedRisks.risk: 🆕 Fadu Gen6 SSD controller + Micron 9650 Gen6 SSD — PCIe Gen6 enterprise ecosystem entering production phase
newEscalatedRisks.status: NEW — COMPUTEX 2026 Day 4. Fadu Sierra FC6161 (28.5GB/s, 512TB, <7W). Micron 9650 (world's first commercial Gen6 SSD).
